verdict: High. Lawyer scores 48 / 100 on AI displacement exposure over a 5-year horizon.

timeline.description: AI-assisted document review and drafting become more prevalent

timeline_callout: AI-assisted tools may increasingly automate routine tasks, but human judgment and expertise remain essential for complex cases
key_tasks: Represent clients in court or before government agencies
Prepare, draft, and review legal documents
reasoning_steps: The high score is due to the significant regulatory moat and physical dexterity required for this occupation.
While AI can assist with document review, human judgment and expertise are essential for complex cases.

quotes.quote: Lawyers who use AI will replace lawyers who do not. The profession is not going away, but the structure of how legal work gets done is going to change in a very significant way.
quotes.speaker: Richard Susskind
quotes.title: Author, Tomorrow's Lawyers; technology adviser to the Lord Chief Justice of England

quotes.quote: Document review, due diligence, and first-draft contract work are the parts of legal practice most exposed. Counseling and courtroom judgment are the most insulated.
quotes.speaker: Daniel Susskind
quotes.title: Author, A World Without Work; Oxford economist

quotes.quote: We expect roughly 44 percent of legal work tasks to be automatable by current LLMs. That does not mean 44 percent fewer lawyers — it means restructured firms.
quotes.speaker: Goldman Sachs Research
quotes.title: Economic research note
quotes.source: Goldman Sachs: AI Exposure of Occupations

recent_developments.headline: AI contract review reaches enterprise adoption
recent_developments.impact: Top-100 law firms report cutting document review hours by 60–80% on standard agreements.

recent_developments.headline: Courts publish guidance on AI in filings
recent_developments.impact: Multiple jurisdictions now require disclosure when AI assists in drafting legal documents.

recent_developments.headline: Paralegal job postings shift toward AI oversight
recent_developments.impact: New listings emphasize prompt review, citation verification, and AI output audit skills.
